`r if (file.exists(file.path(datasets_dir, "firm_data.csv"))) "## Heterogeneity"`
```{r}
# List of companies

temp <-
    df |>
    select(firm, year) |>
    distinct() |>
    add_firm_data() |>
    select(name, country, primary_sics_sector) |>
    distinct() |>
    arrange(primary_sics_sector, name)

display_tbl <- temp |>
    rename(Name = name, Country = country, Sector = primary_sics_sector) |>
    arrange(Sector, Name)

display_tbl <- display_tbl |>
    group_by(Sector) |>
    mutate(row_in_group = row_number()) |>
    ungroup()

paired_tbl <- display_tbl |>
    mutate(pair_id = (row_in_group + 1) %/% 2) |>
    group_by(Sector, pair_id) |>
    summarise(
        Name_1 = first(Name),
        Country_1 = first(Country),
        Name_2 = last(Name),
        Country_2 = last(Country),
        .groups = "drop"
    )

latex_table <- kbl(
    paired_tbl |> select(Name_1, Country_1, Name_2, Country_2),
    format = "latex",
    booktabs = TRUE,
    longtable = TRUE,
    caption = "List of companies by name and country, grouped by SICS sector",
    align = "ll|ll",
    col.names = c("Name", "Country", "Name", "Country"),
    label = "companies"
) |>
    kable_styling(
        latex_options = c("repeat_header"),
        font_size = 9,
        full_width = FALSE,
        position = "center"
    ) |>
    add_header_above(c(" " = 4)) |>
    row_spec(0, bold = TRUE)

industry_groups <- paired_tbl |>
    mutate(row = row_number()) |>
    group_by(Sector) |>
    summarise(start = min(row), end = max(row), .groups = "drop")

for (i in rev(seq_len(nrow(industry_groups)))) {
    latex_table <- latex_table |>
        group_rows(
            group_label = industry_groups$Sector[i],
            start_row = industry_groups$start[i],
            end_row = industry_groups$end[i]
        )
}

# Adjust vertical spacing and inject extra midrules
latex_table <- paste0(
    "\\renewcommand{\\arraystretch}{0.9}\n",
    "\\begingroup\\fontsize{9}{11}\\selectfont\n",
    latex_table,
    "\\endgroup{}"
)

latex_table <- gsub(
    "(\\\\multicolumn\\{4\\}\\{l\\}\\{\\\\textbf\\{[^}]+\\}\\}\\\\\\\\)",
    "\\\\midrule\n\\1\n\\\\midrule",
    latex_table,
    perl = TRUE
)

latex_table <- paste0(
    "\\renewcommand{\\arraystretch}{0.9}\n", # Reduce vertical space
    latex_table
)

writeLines(latex_table, "tab-companies.tex")

```


```{r violin-plot}
# Updated violin plot with top vs bottom 10% (vs middle 80%)
if (file.exists(file.path(datasets_dir, "firm_data.csv"))) {
    esg_rating_map <- c(
        "A+" = 12, "A" = 11, "A-" = 10,
        "B+" = 9,  "B" = 8,  "B-" = 7,
        "C+" = 6,  "C" = 5,  "C-" = 4,
        "D+" = 3,  "D" = 2,  "D-" = 1
    )

    # reverse-coded
    controversy_score_map <- c(
        "A+" = 1, "A" = 2, "A-" = 3,
        "B+" = 4, "B" = 5, "B-" = 6,
        "C+" = 7, "C" = 8, "C-" = 9,
        "D+" = 10, "D" = 11, "D-" = 12
    )

    msci_company_rating_map <- c(
        "AAA" = 7, "AA" = 6, "A" = 5,
        "BBB" = 4, "BB" = 3, "B" = 2,
        "CCC" = 1
    )

    # Base summarization and firm data enrichment
    df2_enriched <- df |>
        group_by(firm, year) |>
        summarize(transp = mean(exists), .groups = "drop") |>
        add_all_firm_data()

    # Continue transformation
    df2 <- df2_enriched |>
        select(
            firm, year, primary_sics_sector, transp,
            mkcapUSD_lagged, salesUSD,
            ref_esg_score, ref_esg_controversies_score_lagged,
            msci_company_rating_lagged,
            reprisk_rating_num_lagged
        ) |>
        mutate(
            ref_esg_score_n = recode(ref_esg_score, !!!esg_rating_map),
            ref_esg_controversies_score_n = recode(ref_esg_controversies_score_lagged, !!!controversy_score_map),
            msci_company_rating_n = recode(msci_company_rating_lagged, !!!msci_company_rating_map)
        )


    make_grouped_df <- function(df, quant_col) {
        df_grouped <- df |>
            # Compute quantile thresholds yearly
            group_by(year) |>
            mutate(
                q10 = quantile(.data[[quant_col]], 0.1, na.rm = TRUE),
                q90 = quantile(.data[[quant_col]], 0.9, na.rm = TRUE),
                group = case_when(
                    is.na(.data[[quant_col]]) ~ NA_character_,
                    .data[[quant_col]] >= q90 ~ "Top 10%",
                    .data[[quant_col]] <= q10 ~ "Bottom 10%",
                    TRUE ~ "Middle 80%"
                )
            ) |>
            ungroup() |>
            select(-q10, -q90)

        return(df_grouped)
    }

    df_mkcap_plot <- make_grouped_df(df2, "mkcapUSD_lagged")
    # df_sales_plot <- make_grouped_df(df2, "salesUSD")
    # df_rating_plot <- make_grouped_df(df2, "ref_esg_score_n")
    df_controversies_plot <- make_grouped_df(df2, "ref_esg_controversies_score_n")
    df_msci_plot <- make_grouped_df(df2, "msci_company_rating_n")
    df_reprisk_rating_plot <- make_grouped_df(df2, "reprisk_rating_num_lagged")
}
```

<!-- ```{r} -->
<!-- # After violin plot data creation -->
<!-- cat("Violin data - reprisk rating summary:\n") -->
<!-- summary(df_reprisk_rating_plot$reprisk_rating_num_lagged) -->
<!-- table(df_reprisk_rating_plot$group) -->
<!-- ``` -->


<!-- ```{r} -->
<!-- # Quick correlation check between ESG metrics -->
<!-- df2 |> -->
<!--     select( -->
<!--         msci_company_rating_n, -->
<!--         reprisk_rating_num_lagged, -->
<!--         ref_esg_controversies_score_n -->
<!--     ) |> -->
<!--     cor(use = "pairwise.complete.obs") -->
<!-- ``` -->


```{r}
if (file.exists(file.path(datasets_dir, "firm_data.csv"))) {
    # Function  to compare means
    compare_means <- function(df, group_var = "group", target_group = "Top 10%", reference_group = "Middle 80%") {
        variable_sym <- sym(group_var)

        x <- df |>
            filter((!!variable_sym) == target_group) |>
            pull(transp) |>
            na.omit()
        y <- df |>
            filter((!!variable_sym) == reference_group) |>
            pull(transp) |>
            na.omit()

        ttest <- t.test(x = x, y = y, alternative = "two.sided")

        stars <- case_when(
            ttest$p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
            ttest$p.value < 0.01 ~ "**",
            ttest$p.value < 0.05 ~ "*",
            TRUE ~ ""
        )

        sprintf("diff = %+.1f p.p.%s", (ttest$estimate[1] - ttest$estimate[2]) * 100, stars)
    }


    # Function to plot violins
    make_violin_plot <- function(df, x_label, bracket_y = c(0.65, 0.7)) {
        df <- df |> drop_na(transp, group) |> 
        filter(group %in% c("Bottom 10%", "Middle 80%", "Top 10%"))

        ggplot(df, aes(x = factor(group, levels = c("Bottom 10%", "Middle 80%", "Top 10%")), y = transp)) +
            geom_violin(aes(fill = group), alpha = 0.5) +
            geom_boxplot(width = 0.1, outlier.shape = NA) +
            scale_fill_manual(values = blue_shades) +
            scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent, limits = c(0, 0.75), expand = c(0, 0)) +
            labs(x = x_label, y = "Transparency score") +
            theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
            theme(
                panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
                panel.border = element_blank(),
                axis.line = element_line(linewidth = 0.25),
                axis.ticks = element_line(linewidth = 0.25),
                legend.position = "none",
                axis.text.y = if (x_label == "Market capitalization") element_text() else element_blank(),
                axis.title.y = if (x_label == "Market capitalization") element_text() else element_blank()
            ) +
            ggpubr::geom_bracket(
                xmin = c("Bottom 10%", "Top 10%"),
                xmax = c("Middle 80%", "Middle 80%"),
                y.position = bracket_y,
                label = c(
                    compare_means(df, "group", "Bottom 10%", "Middle 80%"),
                    compare_means(df, "group", "Top 10%", "Middle 80%")
                )
            )
    }
}
```

```{r}
if (file.exists(file.path(datasets_dir, "firm_data.csv"))) {
    blue_shades <- c(
        "Bottom 10%" = "#bdd7e7",
        "Middle 80%" = "#6baed6",
        "Top 10%" = "#2171b5"
    )

    violin_mkcap <- make_violin_plot(df_mkcap_plot, "Market capitalization")
    # violin_sales <- make_violin_plot(df_sales_plot, "Revenue")
    # violin_rating <- make_violin_plot(df_rating_plot, "ESG rating")
    # violin_controversies <- make_violin_plot(df_controversies_plot, "ESG controversies score")
    violin_msci_rating <- make_violin_plot(df_msci_plot, "ESG rating")
    violin_reprisk_rating <- make_violin_plot(df_reprisk_rating_plot, "RepRisk Rating")

    p <- violin_mkcap + violin_msci_rating + violin_reprisk_rating +
        plot_layout(ncol = 3) +

        plot_annotation(
            tag_levels = "a"
        ) &

        theme(
            plot.tag = element_text(face = "bold"),
            plot.caption = ggtext::element_textbox_simple(hjust = 0),
            plot.caption.position = "plot"
        )

    p

    ggsave(
        here("fig", "fig-heterogeneity-companies.pdf"),
        p,
        width = 9.5, height = 5, dpi = 300, units = "in", device = "pdf"
    )

    knitr::include_graphics(here("fig/fig-heterogeneity-companies.pdf"))
}
```

```{r panel-regression}
# Panel regression
if (file.exists(file.path(datasets_dir, "firm_data.csv"))) {
    # Base summarization and firm data enrichment
    df2_enriched <- df |>
        group_by(firm, year) |>
        summarize(
            transp = mean(exists),
            .groups = "drop"
        ) |>
        add_all_firm_data() |> 
      mutate(
        sector = primary_sics_sector,
        ref_esg_score_n = recode(ref_esg_score, !!!esg_rating_map),
        ref_esg_contr_n = recode(ref_esg_controversies_score_lagged, !!!controversy_score_map),
        msci_company_rating_n = recode(msci_company_rating_lagged, !!!msci_company_rating_map),
        mkcapUSD_log = log(mkcapUSD_lagged)
      ) |> 
      select(firm, year, sector, transp, mkcapUSD_log, msci_company_rating_n, ref_esg_contr_n, reprisk_rating_num_lagged) |> 
      group_by(year) |> 
      mutate(
        # market cap dummies
        q10_mkcap = mkcapUSD_log <= stats::quantile(mkcapUSD_log, 0.10, na.rm = TRUE),
        q90_mkcap = mkcapUSD_log >= stats::quantile(mkcapUSD_log, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE),
    
        # rating dummies
        q10_rating = msci_company_rating_n <= stats::quantile(msci_company_rating_n, 0.10, na.rm = TRUE),
        q90_rating = msci_company_rating_n >= stats::quantile(msci_company_rating_n, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE),
    
        # controversies dummies
        q10_contr = ref_esg_contr_n <= stats::quantile(ref_esg_contr_n, 0.10, na.rm = TRUE),
        q90_contr = ref_esg_contr_n >= stats::quantile(ref_esg_contr_n, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE),
        
        # reprisk rating dummies
        q10_reprisk_rating = reprisk_rating_num_lagged <= stats::quantile(reprisk_rating_num_lagged, 0.10, na.rm = TRUE),
        q90_reprisk_rating = reprisk_rating_num_lagged >= stats::quantile(reprisk_rating_num_lagged, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE)
        
      ) |> 
      ungroup()
}
```


<!-- ```{r} -->

<!-- # After regression data creation   -->
<!-- cat("Regression data - reprisk rating summary:\n") -->
<!-- summary(df2_enriched$reprisk_rating_num_lagged) -->
<!-- table(df2_enriched$q10_reprisk_rating, df2_enriched$q90_reprisk_rating) -->
<!-- ``` -->


```{r}
if (file.exists(file.path(datasets_dir, "firm_data.csv"))) {
  # helper function
  make_panel_tex <- function(data, main, controls, stars, coef_map) {

  # choose focal dummy pair
  main_terms <- switch(
    main,
    "mkcap"  = c("q10_mkcap",  "q90_mkcap"),
    "rating" = c("q10_rating", "q90_rating"),
    #"contr"  = c("q10_contr",  "q90_contr")
    "reprisk" = c("q10_reprisk_rating", "q90_reprisk_rating")
  )

  # build RHS
  rhs_main_only <- paste(main_terms, collapse = " + ")
  rhs_full      <- paste(c(main_terms, controls), collapse = " + ")

  f0 <- stats::as.formula(paste0("transp ~ ", rhs_main_only))
  f1 <- stats::as.formula(paste0("transp ~ ", rhs_full))
  f2 <- stats::as.formula(paste0("transp ~ ", rhs_full, " | year"))
  f3 <- stats::as.formula(paste0("transp ~ ", rhs_full, " | sector"))
  f4 <- stats::as.formula(paste0("transp ~ ", rhs_full, " | sector^year"))
  f5 <- stats::as.formula(paste0("transp ~ ", rhs_full, " | sector^year + firm"))

  models <- list(
    fixest::feols(f0, data = data, vcov = ~firm),
    fixest::feols(f1, data = data, vcov = ~firm),
    fixest::feols(f2, data = data, vcov = ~firm),
    fixest::feols(f3, data = data, vcov = ~firm),
    fixest::feols(f4, data = data, vcov = ~firm),
    fixest::feols(f5, data = data, vcov = ~firm)
  )

  # hide control coefficients
  omit_regex <- paste0("^(", paste(controls, collapse = "|"), ")$")

  modelsummary::modelsummary(
    models,
    stars = stars,
    coef_map = coef_map,
    coef_omit = omit_regex,
    gof_omit = "^(?!.*(Num.Obs.|R2 Adj.|FE))",
    output = "latex",
    escape = FALSE
  ) |>
    as.character()
  }

  # Panel A: market cap dummies; controls are rating + controversies
  panel_a_tex <- make_panel_tex(
    data = df2_enriched,
    main = "mkcap",
    controls = c("msci_company_rating_n", "ref_esg_contr_n"),
    stars = c("***" = .001, "**" = .01, "*" = 0.05),
    coef_map = c(
      "(Intercept)" = "Constant",
      "q90_mkcapTRUE" = "Top-10\\% market capitalization",
      "q10_mkcapTRUE" = "Bottom-10\\% market capitalization"
    )
  )
  
  # Panel B: rating dummies; controls are market cap + controversies
  panel_b_tex <- make_panel_tex(
    data = df2_enriched,
    main = "rating",
    controls = c("mkcapUSD_log", "ref_esg_contr_n"),
    stars = c("***" = .001, "**" = .01, "*" = 0.05),
    coef_map = c(
      "(Intercept)" = "Constant",
      "q90_ratingTRUE" = "Top-10\\% ESG rating",
      "q10_ratingTRUE" = "Bottom-10\\% ESG rating"
    )
  )
  
  # Panel C: reprisk dummies; controls are market cap + rating
  panel_c_tex <- make_panel_tex(
    data = df2_enriched,
    main = "reprisk",
    controls = c("mkcapUSD_log", "msci_company_rating_n"),
    stars = c("***" = .001, "**" = .01, "*" = 0.05),
    coef_map = c(
      "(Intercept)" = "Constant",
      "q90_reprisk_ratingTRUE" = "Top-10\\% RepRisk Rating",
      "q10_reprisk_ratingTRUE" = "Bottom-10\\% RepRisk Rating"
    )
  )

  # helper: robust extraction
  extract_blocks <- function(tex) {
    lines <- unlist(strsplit(tex, "\n"))

    i_mid <- which(grepl("\\\\midrule", lines))[1]
    i_bot <- which(grepl("\\\\bottomrule", lines))[1]
    body  <- lines[(i_mid + 1):(i_bot - 1)]

    # pull GOF lines (Num.Obs. + R2 Adj.) out of body
    gof_lines <- body[grepl("^Num\\.Obs\\.|^R2 Adj\\.", body)]
    # coef block = everything before Num.Obs.
    i_n <- which(grepl("^Num\\.Obs\\.", body))[1]
    coef_block <- if (!is.na(i_n) && i_n > 1) body[1:(i_n - 1)] else body

    list(coef_block = coef_block, gof_lines = gof_lines)
  }

  A <- extract_blocks(panel_a_tex)
  B <- extract_blocks(panel_b_tex)
  C <- extract_blocks(panel_c_tex)
  
  gofA <- A$gof_lines
  gofA <- gsub("Num\\.Obs\\.", "$N$", gofA)
  gofA <- gsub("R2 Adj\\.", "Adj. $R^2$", gofA)
  
  gofB <- B$gof_lines
  gofB <- gsub("Num\\.Obs\\.", "$N$", gofB)
  gofB <- gsub("R2 Adj\\.", "Adj. $R^2$", gofB)
  
  gofC <- C$gof_lines
  gofC <- gsub("Num\\.Obs\\.", "$N$", gofC)
  gofC <- gsub("R2 Adj\\.", "Adj. $R^2$", gofC)

  # build LaTex scaffold
  fe_rows <- c(
  "Controls         & No & Yes & Yes & Yes & Yes & Yes \\\\",
  "Year FE          & No & No  & Yes & No  & --- & --- \\\\",
  "Sector FE        & No & No  & No  & Yes & --- & --- \\\\",
  "Year $\\times$ Sector FE & No & No & No & No & Yes & Yes \\\\",
  "Company FE       & No & No  & No  & No & No  & Yes \\\\"
  )


  footnote_text <- "This table reports regression estimates where the dependent variable is the company-level transparency score, calculated as the number of reported ESG indicators divided by all indicators listed in ESRS. Each panel uses top-10\\% and bottom-10\\% dummies of the focal determinant as regressors. Deciles are calculated on a yearly basis. Panel A reports results using lagged market capitalization as the main independent variable. Panel B uses the lagged ESG rating from MSCI, and panel C uses the lagged RepRisk rating. Model~(1) includes only the focal determinant dummies and no additional controls. Models~(2)–(6) additionally include the remaining two determinants as continuous controls. The models vary by the set of included fixed effects (FE): Model~(1) and Model~(2) include no fixed effects; Model~(3) includes year fixed effects; Model~(4) includes sector fixed effects; Model~(5) includes year-by-sector fixed effects; and Model~(6) includes company fixed effects. Standard errors are clustered by company and reported in parentheses. Significance levels: ***, **, * correspond to 0.1\\%, 1\\%, and 5\\%, respectively. Estimates are based on OLS regression.We find that companies in the top-10\\% (bottom 10\\%) of market capitalization and ESG rating have significantly higher (lower) levels of transparency than the average company. For example, according to Model (1), companies in the top-10\\% versus bottom-10\\% of ESG ratings have transparency scores that are approximately 22\\% higher, calculated by comparing fitted transparency levels from the regression model."

  ltx <- c(
    "\\begin{table}[!htbp]",
    "\\fontsize{9pt}{9pt}\\selectfont\\renewcommand{\\arraystretch}{1.2}",
    "\\caption{\\label{tab:regression-transp-controls}Determinants of transparency}",
    "\\centering",
    "\\begin{threeparttable}",
    "",
    # --- Panel A
    "\\multicolumn{6}{l}{\\textit{Panel A: Transparency explained by market capitalization}} \\\\",
    "\\begin{tabularx}{\\textwidth}{ p{5cm} *{6}{>{\\centering\\arraybackslash}X} }",
    "\\toprule",
    " & (1) & (2) & (3) & (4) & (5) & (6) \\\\",
    "\\midrule",
    A$coef_block,
    "\\midrule",
    fe_rows,
    "\\midrule",
    gofA,
    "\\bottomrule",
    "\\end{tabularx}", "", "\\vspace{1em}",

    # --- Panel B
    "\\multicolumn{6}{l}{\\textit{Panel B: Transparency explained by ESG rating}} \\\\",
    "\\begin{tabularx}{\\textwidth}{ p{5cm} *{6}{>{\\centering\\arraybackslash}X} }",
    "\\toprule",
    " & (1) & (2) & (3) & (4) & (5) & (6) \\\\",
    "\\midrule",
    B$coef_block,
    "\\midrule",
    fe_rows,
    "\\midrule",
    gofB,
    "\\bottomrule",
    "\\end{tabularx}", "", "\\vspace{1em}",

    # --- Panel C
    "\\multicolumn{6}{l}{\\textit{Panel C: Transparency explained by RepRisk rating}} \\\\",
    "\\begin{tabularx}{\\textwidth}{ p{5cm} *{6}{>{\\centering\\arraybackslash}X} }",
    "\\toprule",
    " & (1) & (2) & (3) & (4) & (5) & (6) \\\\",
    "\\midrule",
    C$coef_block,
    "\\midrule",
    fe_rows,
    "\\midrule",
    gofC,
    "\\bottomrule",
    "\\end{tabularx}", "",

    # Notes
    "\\begin{tablenotes}[para, flushleft]",
    "\\scriptsize",
    "\\fontsize{7pt}{7pt}\\linespread{1.2}\\selectfont",
    sprintf("\\item \\textbf{Notes:} %s", footnote_text),
    "\\end{tablenotes}",
    "\\end{threeparttable}",
    "\\end{table}"
  )

  writeLines(paste(ltx, collapse = "\n"), "tab-regression_transp.tex")
}
```

<!-- ```{r panel regression time trend} -->
<!-- if (file.exists(file.path(datasets_dir, "firm_data.csv"))) { -->

<!--   # Build firm-year panel with regressors -->
<!--   df_trend <- df |> -->
<!--     dplyr::group_by(firm, year) |> -->
<!--     dplyr::summarise(transp = mean(exists), .groups = "drop") |> -->
<!--     add_all_firm_data() |> -->
<!--     dplyr::mutate( -->
<!--       sector = primary_sics_sector, -->
<!--       mkcapUSD_log = log(mkcapUSD_lagged), -->
<!--       msci_company_rating_n = dplyr::recode(msci_company_rating_lagged, !!!msci_company_rating_map), -->
<!--       ref_esg_contr_n = dplyr::recode(ref_esg_controversies_score_lagged, !!!controversy_score_map) -->
<!--     ) |> -->
<!--     dplyr::select(firm, year, sector, country, transp, mkcapUSD_log, msci_company_rating_n, ref_esg_contr_n) -->

<!--   # Spec (1): year FE + sector FE + country FE -->
<!--   m1 <- fixest::feols( -->
<!--     transp ~ mkcapUSD_log + msci_company_rating_n + ref_esg_contr_n | year + sector + country, -->
<!--     data = df_trend, -->
<!--     vcov = ~ firm -->
<!--   ) -->

<!--   # Spec (2): year FE + firm FE -->
<!--   m2 <- fixest::feols( -->
<!--     transp ~ mkcapUSD_log + msci_company_rating_n + ref_esg_contr_n | year + firm, -->
<!--     data = df_trend, -->
<!--     vcov = ~ firm -->
<!--   ) -->

<!--   # Spec (3): sector × year FE  (no separate year FE) -->
<!--   m3 <- fixest::feols( -->
<!--     transp ~ mkcapUSD_log + msci_company_rating_n + ref_esg_contr_n | sector^year, -->
<!--     data = df_trend, -->
<!--     vcov = ~ firm -->
<!--   ) -->

<!--   # output table -->
<!--   tex_raw <- modelsummary::modelsummary( -->
<!--     list( -->
<!--       "Sector+Country FE" = m1, -->
<!--       "Firm FE" = m2, -->
<!--       "Sector$\\times$Year FE" = m3 -->
<!--     ), -->
<!--     stars = c("***" = .01, "**" = .05, "*" = 0.1), -->
<!--     coef_map = c( -->
<!--       "mkcapUSD_log" = "Log market cap (lagged)", -->
<!--       "msci_company_rating_n" = "ESG rating (lagged)", -->
<!--       "ref_esg_contr_n" = "ESG controversies (lagged)" -->
<!--     ), -->
<!--     gof_omit = ".*", -->
<!--     output = "latex", -->
<!--   ) |> -->
<!--     as.character() -->

<!--   tex_lines <- unlist(strsplit(tex_raw, "\n")) -->

<!--   # extract coefficient block between \midrule and \bottomrule -->
<!--   i_mid <- which(grepl("\\\\midrule", tex_lines))[1] -->
<!--   i_bot <- which(grepl("\\\\bottomrule", tex_lines))[1] -->
<!--   coef_block <- tex_lines[(i_mid + 1):(i_bot - 1)] -->

<!--   # compute GOF -->
<!--   N1 <- stats::nobs(m1); N2 <- stats::nobs(m2); N3 <- stats::nobs(m3) -->
<!--   ar2_1 <- unname(fixest::fitstat(m1, "ar2"))  # adjusted R2 -->
<!--   ar2_2 <- unname(fixest::fitstat(m2, "ar2")) -->
<!--   ar2_3 <- unname(fixest::fitstat(m3, "ar2")) -->

<!--   # format numbers -->
<!--   fmtN  <- function(x) format(x, big.mark = ",", scientific = FALSE, trim = TRUE) -->
<!--   fmtR2 <- function(x) sprintf("%.3f", x) -->

<!--   # FE rows -->
<!--   fe_rows <- c( -->
<!--     "\\midrule", -->
<!--     sprintf("Year FE            & %s & %s & %s \\\\", "Yes", "Yes", "Yes"), -->
<!--     sprintf("Sector FE          & %s & %s & %s \\\\", "Yes", "No",  "---"), -->
<!--     sprintf("Country FE         & %s & %s & %s \\\\", "Yes", "No",  "No"), -->
<!--     sprintf("Firm FE            & %s & %s & %s \\\\", "No",  "Yes", "No"), -->
<!--     sprintf("Sector $\\times$ Year FE & %s & %s & %s \\\\", "No", "No", "Yes"), -->
<!--     "\\midrule", -->
<!--     sprintf("$N$               & %s & %s & %s \\\\", fmtN(N1), fmtN(N2), fmtN(N3)), -->
<!--     sprintf("Adj. $R^2$        & %s & %s & %s \\\\", fmtR2(ar2_1), fmtR2(ar2_2), fmtR2(ar2_3)) -->
<!--   ) -->

<!--   footnote_text <- paste0( -->
<!--     "This table reports firm-year panel regressions where the dependent variable is the company-level transparency score. ", -->
<!--     "Regressors are log market capitalization (lagged), lagged ESG rating, and lagged ESG controversies score. ", -->
<!--     "All specifications include year fixed effects. Model (1) adds sector and country fixed effects. ", -->
<!--     "Model (2) adds firm fixed effects. Model (3) includes sector-by-year fixed effects. ", -->
<!--     "Standard errors are clustered by compnay and reported in parantheses. Significance levels: ***, **, * correspond to 1\\%, 5\\%, and 10\\%." -->
<!--   ) -->

<!--   # table scaffold -->
<!--   ltx <- c( -->
<!--     "\\begin{table}[!htbp]", -->
<!--     "\\fontsize{9pt}{9pt}\\selectfont\\renewcommand{\\arraystretch}{1.2}", -->
<!--     "\\caption{\\label{tab:regression-transp-controls}Transparency and firm characteristics}", -->
<!--     "\\centering", -->
<!--     "\\begin{threeparttable}", -->
<!--     "", -->
<!--     "\\begin{tabularx}{\\textwidth}{ p{5cm} ccc }", -->
<!--     "\\toprule", -->
<!--     " & Sector+Country FE & Firm FE & Sector$\\times$Year FE \\\\", -->
<!--     "\\midrule", -->
<!--     coef_block, -->
<!--     fe_rows, -->
<!--     "\\bottomrule", -->
<!--     "\\end{tabularx}", -->
<!--     "", -->
<!--     "\\begin{tablenotes}[para, flushleft]", -->
<!--     "\\scriptsize", -->
<!--     "\\fontsize{10pt}{10pt}\\linespread{1.2}\\selectfont", -->
<!--     sprintf("\\item \\textbf{Notes:} %s", footnote_text), -->
<!--     "\\end{tablenotes}", -->
<!--     "\\end{threeparttable}", -->
<!--     "\\end{table}" -->
<!--   ) -->

<!--   out_tex <- here::here("analyses/chunks/tab-regression_transp_trend_controls.tex") -->
<!--   writeLines(paste(ltx, collapse = "\n"), out_tex) -->
<!-- } -->

<!-- ``` -->

<!-- ```{r} -->
<!-- # Plot year fixed effects from the firm-FE model (m2) -->

<!-- # helper -->
<!-- `%||%` <- function(a, b) if (!is.null(a)) a else b -->

<!-- # extract year FE -->
<!-- fe_obj <- tryCatch(fixest::fixef(m2, se = TRUE), error = function(e) NULL) -->

<!-- if (!is.null(fe_obj) && !is.null(fe_obj$year) && is.data.frame(fe_obj$year)) { -->
<!--   # data.frame with rownames = years -->
<!--   yr_df <- fe_obj$year -->

<!--   # pick estimate / se columns -->
<!--   est_col <- intersect(c("fixef", "Estimate", "estimate"), names(yr_df)) -->
<!--   if (length(est_col) == 0) est_col <- names(yr_df)[1] -->

<!--   se_col <- intersect(c("se", "Std. Error", "Std..Error", "std.error"), names(yr_df)) -->
<!--   if (length(se_col) == 0) se_col <- character(0) -->

<!--   est <- yr_df[[est_col[1]]] -->
<!--   se  <- if (length(se_col) > 0) yr_df[[se_col[1]]] else rep(NA_real_, length(est)) -->

<!--   names(est) <- rownames(yr_df) -->
<!--   names(se)  <- rownames(yr_df) -->
<!-- } else { -->
<!--   # fallback: estimates only -->
<!--   est <- fixest::fixef(m2)$year -->
<!--   se  <- NULL -->
<!-- } -->

<!-- # robust year parsing -->
<!-- parse_year <- function(x) as.integer(gsub("^.*?(\\d{4}).*$", "\\1", x)) -->

<!-- year_fe <- tibble::tibble( -->
<!--   year = parse_year(names(est)), -->
<!--   fe   = as.numeric(est), -->
<!--   se   = if (!is.null(se)) as.numeric(se) else NA_real_ -->
<!-- ) |> -->
<!--   dplyr::filter(!is.na(year)) |> -->
<!--   dplyr::arrange(year) -->

<!-- # normalize to base year -->
<!-- base_year <- min(year_fe$year, na.rm = TRUE) -->
<!-- base_fe   <- year_fe$fe[year_fe$year == base_year][1] -->

<!-- year_fe <- year_fe |> -->
<!--   dplyr::mutate( -->
<!--     fe_rel = fe - base_fe,           # year FE relative to base year -->
<!--     fe_pp  = 100 * fe_rel,           # transparency is share -> percentage points -->
<!--     lo_pp  = 100 * (fe_rel - 1.96 * se), -->
<!--     hi_pp  = 100 * (fe_rel + 1.96 * se) -->
<!--   ) -->

<!-- # if SEs are missing, re-estimate auxiliary model with year dummies -->
<!-- if (all(is.na(year_fe$se)) || nrow(year_fe) <= 1) { -->

<!--   data_m2 <- if (exists("df_trend")) df_trend else tryCatch(fixest::get_data(m2), error = function(e) NULL) -->
<!--   if (is.null(data_m2)) stop("Need df_trend in the environment (the data used for m2).") -->

<!--   # make year numeric -->
<!--   data_m2 <- data_m2 |> -->
<!--     dplyr::mutate(year = as.integer(as.character(year))) -->

<!--   base_year <- min(data_m2$year, na.rm = TRUE) -->

<!--   # Auxiliary model: firm FE + explicit year dummies; same controls and clustering -->
<!--   m2_aux <- fixest::feols( -->
<!--     transp ~ mkcapUSD_log + msci_company_rating_n + ref_esg_contr_n + fixest::i(year, ref = base_year) | firm, -->
<!--     data = data_m2, -->
<!--     vcov = ~ firm -->
<!--   ) -->

<!--   co <- stats::coef(m2_aux) -->
<!--   vc <- stats::vcov(m2_aux) -->

<!--   idx <- grep("year::", names(co)) -->
<!--   if (length(idx) == 0) { -->
<!--     idx <- grep("\\d{4}", names(co)) -->
<!--   } -->

<!--   yrs <- parse_year(names(co)[idx]) -->
<!--   keep <- !is.na(yrs) -->

<!--   est2 <- co[idx][keep] -->
<!--   se2  <- sqrt(diag(vc))[idx][keep] -->
<!--   yrs  <- yrs[keep] -->

<!--   # build full series incl. base year = 0 -->
<!--   year_fe <- tibble::tibble( -->
<!--     year = c(base_year, yrs), -->
<!--     fe_pp = 100 * c(0, as.numeric(est2)), -->
<!--     se_pp = 100 * c(NA_real_, as.numeric(se2)) -->
<!--   ) |> -->
<!--     dplyr::arrange(year) |> -->
<!--     dplyr::mutate( -->
<!--       lo_pp = fe_pp - 1.96 * se_pp, -->
<!--       hi_pp = fe_pp + 1.96 * se_pp -->
<!--     ) -->

<!--   year_fe <- year_fe |> -->
<!--     dplyr::mutate(se = se_pp / 100) |> -->
<!--     dplyr::select(year, fe_pp, lo_pp, hi_pp, se) -->
<!-- } -->


<!-- p_yearfe <- ggplot2::ggplot(year_fe, ggplot2::aes(x = year, y = fe_pp)) + -->
<!--   { if (any(!is.na(year_fe$se))) ggplot2::geom_ribbon( -->
<!--       ggplot2::aes(ymin = lo_pp, ymax = hi_pp), -->
<!--       alpha = 0.15, -->
<!--       na.rm = TRUE -->
<!--     ) } + -->
<!--   ggplot2::geom_line(linewidth = 1.1) + -->
<!--   ggplot2::geom_point(size = 2, stroke = 0.8, shape = 21, fill = "white") + -->
<!--   ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(year_fe$year), max(year_fe$year), by = 2)) + -->
<!--   ggplot2::labs( -->
<!--     title = paste0("Year effects from firm-FE model (base year = ", base_year, ")"), -->
<!--     y = NULL, x = NULL, color = NULL, fill = NULL -->
<!--   ) + -->
<!--   ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 10) + -->
<!--   ggplot2::theme( -->
<!--     plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(hjust = 0.5), -->
<!--     axis.line = ggplot2::element_line(color = "black"), -->
<!--     axis.ticks = ggplot2::element_line(color = "black"), -->
<!--     panel.grid.major.y = ggplot2::element_line(color = "grey80", linetype = "dashed"), -->
<!--     panel.grid.minor.y = ggplot2::element_blank(), -->
<!--     panel.grid.major.x = ggplot2::element_blank(), -->
<!--     panel.grid.minor.x = ggplot2::element_blank() -->
<!--   ) -->


<!-- ggplot2::ggsave( -->
<!--   filename = here::here("fig", "fig-yearFE-firmFE.pdf"), -->
<!--   plot = p_yearfe, -->
<!--   width = 6, height = 3.5, units = "in", dpi = 400, device = grDevices::pdf -->
<!-- ) -->

<!-- ``` -->

```{r}
if (file.exists(file.path(datasets_dir, "firm_data.csv"))) {

  # ----------------------------
  # options: "rating", "mkcap", "contr", "reprisk"
  det_choice <- "rating"
  # ----------------------------

  # build firm-year panel
  df_trend <- df |>
    dplyr::group_by(firm, year) |>
    dplyr::summarise(transp = mean(exists), .groups = "drop") |>
    add_all_firm_data() |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      sector = primary_sics_sector,
      mkcapUSD_log = log(mkcapUSD_lagged),
      msci_company_rating_n = dplyr::recode(msci_company_rating_lagged, !!!msci_company_rating_map),
      ref_esg_contr_n = dplyr::recode(ref_esg_controversies_score_lagged, !!!controversy_score_map)
    ) |>
    dplyr::select(firm, year, sector, country, transp, mkcapUSD_log, msci_company_rating_n, ref_esg_contr_n, reprisk_rating_num_lagged) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      t = year - min(year, na.rm = TRUE)
    )
  
  # pick suitable controls
  controls <- c("mkcapUSD_log", "reprisk_rating_num_lagged")

  # pick variable used to form q10/q90 groups
  det_var <- switch(
    det_choice,
    "rating"  = "msci_company_rating_n",
    "mkcap"   = "mkcapUSD_log",
    "contr"   = "ref_esg_contr_n",
    "reprisk" = "reprisk_rating_num_lagged"
  )

  # within-year q10/q90 dummies for chosen determinant
  df_trend <- df_trend |>
    dplyr::group_by(year) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      q10 = .data[[det_var]] <= stats::quantile(.data[[det_var]], 0.10, na.rm = TRUE),
      q90 = .data[[det_var]] >= stats::quantile(.data[[det_var]], 0.90, na.rm = TRUE)
    ) |>
    dplyr::ungroup()

  # models
  
  # (1) no controls, no FE
  m1 <- fixest::feols(
    transp ~ q10 + q90 + t + q10:t + q90:t,
    data = df_trend,
    vcov = ~ firm
  )
  
  # (2) add controls
  m2 <- fixest::feols(
    transp ~ q10 + q90 + t + q10:t + q90:t + mkcapUSD_log + reprisk_rating_num_lagged,
    data = df_trend,
    vcov = ~ firm
  )
  
  # (3) add sector FE
  m3 <- fixest::feols(
    transp ~ q10 + q90 + t + q10:t + q90:t + mkcapUSD_log + reprisk_rating_num_lagged | sector,
    data = df_trend,
    vcov = ~ firm
  )
  
  # (4) add firm FE
  m4 <- fixest::feols(
    transp ~ q10 + q90 + t + q10:t + q90:t + mkcapUSD_log + reprisk_rating_num_lagged | firm,
    data = df_trend,
    vcov = ~ firm
  )

  # table body from modelsummary
  coef_map <- c(
    "(Intercept)" = "Constant",
     "q90TRUE" = "Top-10\\% group",
    "q10TRUE" = "Bottom-10\\% group",
    "t" = "Time trend",
    "q90TRUE:t" = "Top-10\\% $\\times$ Time trend",
    "q10TRUE:t" = "Bottom-10\\% $\\times$ Time trend"
  )

  tex_raw <- modelsummary::modelsummary(
    list(
      "(1)" = m1,
      "(2)" = m2,
      "(3)" = m3,
      "(4)" = m4
    ),
    stars = c("***" = .001, "**" = .01, "*" = 0.05),
    coef_map = coef_map,
    coef_omit = paste0("^(", paste(controls, collapse = "|"), ")$"),
    gof_omit = ".*",
    output = "latex",
    escape = FALSE
  ) |>
    as.character()


  tex_lines <- unlist(strsplit(tex_raw, "\n"))
  i_mid <- which(grepl("\\\\midrule", tex_lines))[1]
  i_bot <- which(grepl("\\\\bottomrule", tex_lines))[1]
  coef_block <- tex_lines[(i_mid + 1):(i_bot - 1)]
  
  # GOF
  Ns  <- sapply(list(m1, m2, m3, m4), stats::nobs)
  ar2 <- sapply(list(m1, m2, m3, m4), function(m) unname(fixest::fitstat(m, "ar2")))

  fmtN  <- function(x) format(x, big.mark = ",", scientific = FALSE, trim = TRUE)
  fmtR2 <- function(x) sprintf("%.3f", x)

  det_label <- switch(
    det_choice,
    "rating"  = "ESG rating",
    "mkcap"   = "market capitalization",
    "contr"   = "ESG controversies",
    "reprisk" = "RepRisk rating"
  )

  fe_rows <- c(
    "\\midrule",
    sprintf("Determinant for top/bottom groups & \\multicolumn{4}{c}{%s} \\\\", det_label),
    sprintf("Controls               & No & Yes & Yes & Yes \\\\"),
    sprintf("Sector FE              & No & No  & Yes & No  \\\\"),
    sprintf("Firm FE                & No & No  & No  & Yes \\\\"),
    "\\midrule",
    sprintf("$N$                     & %s & %s & %s & %s \\\\",
            fmtN(Ns[1]), fmtN(Ns[2]), fmtN(Ns[3]), fmtN(Ns[4])),
    sprintf("Adj. $R^2$              & %s & %s & %s & %s \\\\",
            fmtR2(ar2[1]), fmtR2(ar2[2]), fmtR2(ar2[3]), fmtR2(ar2[4]))
  )

  footnote_text <- paste0(
    "This table reports firm-year panel regressions where the dependent variable is the company-level transparency score, calculated as the number of reported ESG indicators divided by all indicators listed in ESRS. The specification includes top-10\\% and bottom-10\\% dummies of the lagged ESG rating from MSCI, a linear time trend, and interactions between the time trend and the group dummies as regressors. Deciles are calculated on a yearly basis. Model~(1) includes no additional controls or fixed effects. Models~(2)-(4) additionally include lagged market capitalization and the lagged RepRisk rating as continous controls. Model~(3) includes sector fixed effects. Model~(4) incudes firm fixed effects. Standard errors are clustered at the firm level and reported in parenthesese. Significance levels: ***, **, * correspond to 0.1\\%, 1\\%, and 5\\%, respectively. Estimates are based on OLS regression."
    )

  ltx <- c(
    "\\begin{table}[!htbp]",
    "\\fontsize{9pt}{9pt}\\selectfont\\renewcommand{\\arraystretch}{1.2}",
    "\\caption{\\label{tab:regression-transp-trend-heterogeneity}Transparency trends by rating group}",
    "\\centering",
    "\\begin{threeparttable}",
    "",
    "\\begin{tabularx}{\\textwidth}{ p{5cm} cccc }",
    "\\toprule",
    " & (1) & (2) & (3) & (4) \\\\",
    "\\midrule",
    coef_block,
    fe_rows,
    "\\bottomrule",
    "\\end{tabularx}",
    "",
    "\\begin{tablenotes}[para, flushleft]",
    "\\scriptsize",
    "\\fontsize{7pt}{7pt}\\linespread{1.2}\\selectfont",
    sprintf("\\item \\textbf{Notes:} %s", footnote_text),
    "\\end{tablenotes}",
    "\\end{threeparttable}",
    "\\end{table}"
  )

  out_tex <- here::here("analyses/chunks/tab-regression_transp_trend_interactions.tex")
  writeLines(paste(ltx, collapse = "\n"), out_tex)
}

```
